alignments.preprint_claim_text: ReLU networks with width $\text{O}(\text{max}\{d\lfloor N^{1/d}\rfloor, N+2\})$ and depth $\text{O}(L)$ can approximate a H"older continuous function on $[0,1]^d$ with an approximation rate $\text{O}(\lambda\sqrt{d} (N^2L^2\ln N)^{-\alpha/d}). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ReLU networks with width O(max{d⌊N1/d⌋, N + 2}) and depth O(L) can approximate a Hölder continuous function on [0, 1]d with an approximation rate O(λd(N2L2lnN)−α/d). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The structural claim is the same but the published approximation rate changes the dependence on d (from √d to d), a substantive mathematical revision while keeping the same scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approximation rate achieved is optimal up to a constant in terms of width and depth separately. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For an arbitrary continuous function $f$ on $[0,1]^d$, the approximation rate becomes $\text{O}(\sqrt{d}\,\omega_f((N^2L^2\ln N)^{-1/d})), where \omega_f(\cdot) is the modulus of continuity. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approximation rate for an arbitrary continuous function f on [0, 1]d becomes O(dωf((N2L2lnN)−1/d)). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The functional form is the same but the published prefactor changes from √d to d, indicating a mathematical revision of the approximation constant.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using ReLU networks with depth 31 and width $\text{O}(N)$ to approximate one-dimensional Lipschitz continuous functions on $[0,1]$ results in an approximation rate of $\text{O}(\frac{\lambda}{W\ln W})$, where $W=\text{O}(N^2)$. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approximation rate for one-dimensional Lipschitz continuous functions on [0, 1] using ReLU networks with depth 31 and width O(N) becomes O(λWlnW). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a materially different asymptotic form (multiplicative rather than reciprocal), so the numerical/formulaic claim was revised substantially.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates that multi-task models can be effectively trained using approaches such as multi-task learning with separate task heads, back-translation, and multi-lingual training.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research indicates that Transformer based models can perform differently and better in certain instances for hate speech identification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The research identifies that Transformer based models can perform differently and better in certain instances compared to other models.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding about Transformer models performing differently and sometimes better, with only minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is possible to utilize different combined approaches to obtain models that generalize easily across different languages and tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed models can generalize easily across different languages and tasks while sacrificing some accuracy for reduced inference time compute cost.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the generalization claim but adds an explicit accuracy vs. inference-time trade-off, which tempers certainty and clarifies performance characteristics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study shows a trade-off between slight accuracy and reduced inference time compute cost when using the proposed models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed models can generalize easily across different languages and tasks while sacrificing some accuracy for reduced inference time compute cost.
alignments.rationale: Both statements describe the same trade-off between some accuracy loss and reduced inference compute; the publication nests this with generalization but preserves the trade-off claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The updated version of the HASOC 2019 code with new improvements has been made open source.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a new contribution (open-sourcing updated code) that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Optimal default hyperparameter values for one-class classification methods have been determined across 246 problems from 50 real-world datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Optimal default hyperparameter values for one-class classification methods have been determined across 246 problems from 50 real-world datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact contribution about optimal default hyperparameters across the same datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new data descriptor, Average Localised Proximity (ALP), addresses issues with existing approaches based on nearest neighbour distances.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new data descriptor, Average Localised Proximity (ALP), addresses issues with existing approaches based on nearest neighbour distances.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the ALP descriptor and its motivation without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ALP outperforms the Isolation Forest and several other data descriptors in classification performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ALP outperforms the Isolation Forest and several other data descriptors in classification performance.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim for ALP versus Isolation Forest and others is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is weak evidence that ALP outperforms Support Vector Machine in classification performance.

alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same cautious claim about weak evidence versus SVM.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The convolutional neural network developed in this study can identify planetary transit signals and dismiss false positives without requiring prior transit parameters.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core result about the CNN detecting transits and rejecting false positives without prior parameters.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network performs inference on a TESS 30-minute cadence light curve in approximately 5 milliseconds on a single GPU.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network performs inference on a TESS 30-minute-cadence light curve in approximately 5 milliseconds on a single GPU.
alignments.rationale: The inference-time performance claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study presents 181 new planet candidates identified by the neural network, which have passed subsequent human vetting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The USP test can detect distributions that violate independence in only a minimal way.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The USP test can detect distributions that violate independence in only a minimal way.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The power of the USP test can be dramatically greater than those of Pearson's test and the G-test.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Offline testing results cannot be exploited to reduce the cost of online testing in practice. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this broad contextual claim about HAR; it focuses on the B-HAR framework instead, so the general claim from the preprint is not carried forward.

alignments.rationale: The explicit review/contribution statement from the preprint does not appear among the publication claims provided, so it was not carried forward into the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SelfHAR shows an increase of up to 12% in F1 score using the same number of model parameters at inference compared to previous approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SelfHAR achieves up to a 12% increase in F1 score while using the same number of model parameters at inference. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SelfHAR is data-efficient, reaching similar performance using up to 10 times less labeled data compared to supervised approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SelfHAR is data-efficient, reaching similar performance using up to 10 times less labeled data compared to supervised approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The work sheds light on how pre-training tasks may affect downstream performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieved a Dice similarity coefficient of 94.79 percent for individual 3D tooth segmentation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method provides an effective clinical and practical framework for digital dentistry. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
